Ocean Monitoring Indicator (OMI) of the Copernicus 
Marine Environment Monitoring Service 
BLKSEA_OMI_HEALTH_trend 
 
 
 Issue: 1.3 
Contributors: Arthur Capet, Luc Vandenbulcke, Marilaure Grégoire  
Approval date by the CMEMS product quality coordination team: November 2022 
 
 
 
 
  
 
QUID for CMEMS OMIs: 
BLKSEA_OMI_HEALTH_OXYGEN_trend 
Ref:  CMEMS-OMI-QUID-BLKSEA-OMI-HEALTH-
OXYGEN-TREND 
Date: 10/06/2022 
Issue: 1.3 
 
 
     Page 2/ 5 
CHANGE RECORD 
When the quality of the products changes, the QuID is updated and a row is added to this table.  The 
third column specifies which sections or sub -sections have been updated.  The fourth column should  
mention the version of the product to which the change applies. 
 
Issue Date § Description of Change Author Validated By 
1.0 19/02/2019 all Creation of the document Arthur Capet  
1.1 13/10/2020 all Added figures Arthur Capet  
1.2 18/08/2020 All 2020 Update Arthur Capet  
1.3 10/06/2022 All 2022 Update Arthur Capet  
 
 
 
 
Products name:  
BLKSEA_OMI_HEALTH_OXYGEN_trend 
Indicator evaluation method:  
The oxygenation status of the Black Sea open basin is monitored through three complementary 
indicators, that are derived from vertical profiles of oxygen concentration, salinity and temperature 
(Capet et al, 2016). 
(1) The oxygen penetration depth is defined as the depth below which oxygen concentrations are 
below 20 mmol/m³ (Fig. 1). This threshold is chose n to minimize the sensitivity of the indicators to the 
accuracy of different sensors at very low oxygen concentrations. 
(2) The oxygen penetration density is defined as the potential density anomaly at the oxygen 
penetration depth (Fig. 2). Using pycnal vertical coordinates minimizes the uncertainty stemming from 
the spatial variability of oxygen penetration depth, currently undersampled by the limited number of 
Argo profilers. It is considered that this spatial variability, combined with the limited spatia l cover of 
Argo sampling is the main source of error in the basin average. 
QUID for CMEMS OMIs: 
BLKSEA_OMI_HEALTH_OXYGEN_trend 
Ref:  CMEMS-OMI-QUID-BLKSEA-OMI-HEALTH-
OXYGEN-TREND 
Date: 10/06/2022 
Issue: 1.3 
 
 
     Page 3/ 5 
 
(3) The oxygen inventory is the vertical integral of oxygen concentration (Fig 3.). This metric is sensitive 
to other aspect of the oxygen dynamics, such as decrease in the surfac e oxygen content due to limited 
oxygen solubility in warm conditions.  
 
Figure 1 : Oxygen penetration depth in the open Black Sea. Upper panel: Annual trends 
from ship -cast (1955 -2005) and autonomous platforms (Argo, 2012 -2021) in -situ 
observations. Lower Panel: Monthly trends from Argo in -situ observations. Shaded area 
represent the standard error. 
Each metric is extracted from individual Argo profiles, and used to compute monthly and annual 
averages, for the Argo Era (2012 -2021). In addition, historical s hip casts are gathered to extract 
interannual trend for each metric using the DIVA detrending protocol (Capet et al, 2014). 
Scientific validation: 
The methodology to define and estimate those indicators on the basis of in -situ ship-casts is presented 
and d iscussed extensively in (Capet et al, 2016). Ship -cast are processed with the DIVA detrending 
protocol (Capet et al, 2014) to dissociate spatial variability from seasonnal and interannual trends. This 
methodology is validated on the basis of synthetic data sets and by considering the better documented 
case of the mixed layer depth. Years with insufficient data coverage were discarded from the trend 
analyses. The results are concomittant with similar descriptions gathered from independent litterature as 
concern the oxygenation trends (eg. Konovalov et al., 2001, Pakhomova et al., 2014 ) and modern 
oxygen conditions (eg. Stanev et al, 2013). Additional details can be found in the CMEMS Ocean State 
Report (Capet et al., 2018). 
 
 

QUID for CMEMS OMIs:

Figure 2: Potential density anomaly at the oxygen penetration depth in the open Black Sea.  
Upper panel: Annual trends from ship -cast (1955-2005) and autonomous platforms (Argo, 
2012-2021) in -situ observations. Lower Panel: Monthly trends from Argo in -situ 
observations. Shaded area represent the standard error. 
 
 
Figure 3: Oxygen inventory in the open Black Sea.  Upper panel: Annual trends from ship -
cast (1955-2005) and autonomous platforms (Argo, 2012 -2021) in-situ observations. Lower 
Panel: Monthly trends from all Argo in -situ obse rvations. Shaded area represent the 
standard error.

catalog_entry.summary: This document is a Quality Indicator Document (QUID) for the BLKSEA_OMI_HEALTH_OXYGEN_trend product, detailing the methodology and validation of oxygen indicators in the Black Sea.

variables.long_name: Oxygen concentration in the water column
